So local SEO when we search for something that Google thinks is a local search and again what Google thinks is a local search
So in this case, it's plumber Houston, Texas on top. We have ads. They're labeled sponsored
And just below the ads we have the map results the map pack the Google business listings
Whatever you want to call it and below that we have the organic results if you do if you have this map results show up
Google think it's a local search if you don't have this map show up Google thinks it's a non-local search
Okay, so that's really the difference on the search engine results page the SERP between local and non-local
So the local algorithm Google says it's a blended algorithm
It's based both on your Google business profile and your website
But it's important to note your GBP your Google business profile is what is shown to searchers
So these are three Google business profiles. These are not the websites
you can see they link to the websites, but they are the actual Google business profile and
When this map is shown we can bet that 60 to 70 percent of the traffic of the clicks of the calls of the engagement is
Going to go to one of these three listings
So we also know that roughly five to ten percent of the traffic goes to the ads
So if you don't have a GBP you're trying to rank organically you're already going for 20 to 30 percent of the search volume for that term
75 percent of it's already gone between the map results and the ads. Okay, so that's the local search now a
huge
important
Ranking factor for local search is the distance between your GBP address and where the searcher is
For whatever reason Google wants to show searchers
GBPs that are close
Even though if you're a service-based business like a plumber or an HVAC or a roofer
It doesn't really matter if you'll be willing to drive there Google still will show
GBPs that are closer first
So when we talk about local SEO what that really means is we're trying to build enough trust and relevance with Google's
Algorithm to overcome that distance penalty and rank farther and farther from the GBP address
But it's important to keep in mind
Whatever city your GBP is in it is very very hard to rank in a different city
So if you're in a suburb of Chicago, but you want to rank in Chicago, it's going to be almost impossible to do that
It's very very hard unless you're in a space with almost no competition to rank in the city other than the city that your address is in
Okay, so one trick around this
I know there are a lot of suburbs where you can use a different suburbs name and the mail still comes to you
So one way around this is to change the GBP city to match where you want to rank
Assuming Google still identifies the same location, right?
If you're in Aurora and you try to put Chicago in that won't work
But I know there are a lot of suburbs where the city name is sort of interchangeable for that address
So when we look at local SEO and local progress
We clearly rank track based on distance from the GBP location
So that's where we get rank maps like this because we care not just where we ranking
But where are we ranking for this search location?
So we end up with maps like this. This is just something I pulled from one of our clients
And this is of course what we want to do turn all these reds and yellows to green
I'll show you this tool in another video how to set it up and what it is
So the last thing I want to touch on is ranking factors for local SEO that you have control over
Obviously, I'm going to assume you don't have control over your GBP address
You don't have control over where your searcher is so focusing on
Factors that you have control over and this isn't really in order of strength
It's more like an order of strength slash
Difficulty so something is pretty weak but takes five minutes to do it's going to be on top
Okay, if something is super strong
For ranking, but it's really hard to do it might be lower on the list
So this is sort of like the list of things you should do in the order that you should do a man
Okay, so and a lot of these we're going to cover in more detail as we go through this
But primary GBP category in your landing page title. Oh, please do that
Your GBP needs to be complete every single box and the GBP needs to be filled out your city name should be in your website
landing page title
website
land
Landing page title so we want the primary category and the city name in the landing page title
We want to make sure you have additional GBP categories and services so many local business owners
Don't do this
It's one of the most common things that we do when we get a new client
They have one primary category no secondary categories no services
We want to do five GBP categories and 20 to 30 services
We'll talk more about how to do that spoiler alert chat GPT is going to help us a lot
We want the GBP to be very consistent with the website for categories and services
We'll talk about how to structure that how to make sure that happens the website structure itself
Of course the content the schema that's all very important. You have a lot of control over this
It's a little bit harder to do but you only need to do it one time
Citations press release these used to be more important in the past than they are now
But again, we only have to do them once they're not super time-consuming not super expensive
So you should just do them back links are hugely important, but they're also expensive
That's why they're down here
Reviews are less important to rank than you might think but once you're ranked reviews are incredibly important to actually get clicks to get calls
So they're very very important, especially once you get ranked, but they're kind of hard to do so they're down here
Social signals super easy
But I don't know how important they are and then there's a bunch of other things which again will touch on as you go through the rest of this

summary: Local SEO focuses on optimizing a business's online presence to appear in local search results. It involves building trust and relevance with Google’s algorithm while overcoming the distance penalty for ranking closer to the searcher’s location.
key_points: Google considers both your Google Business Profile (GBP) and website in its blended local algorithm.
Distance from the GBP address significantly impacts local search rankings, making it challenging to rank in a different city.
Completeness and consistency of the GBP profile are crucial for better visibility.
